sv.ref: Baldwin — Practical Guide to Sous Vide (tough-cut window)
sv.note: Baldwin tough-cut window 55-80°C: '131°F (55°C) water bath for 24-48 hours' up to '176°F (80°C)... 12-24 hours'. 74°C/24h sits inside this range (exact 74°C is a design point, not a single quoted figure).

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — Smoked Lamb Ribs / pulled-lamb guidance
smoke.note: Combo hot-finish smoke ~2h; canonical pit 'about 225°F (107°C)', 105°C is within range. tgt 95°C=203°F matches AmazingRibs pull-tender guidance (~200°F). Wood: 'milder woods like cherry or pecan add a subtle smoke flavor.'

smoke_only.note: 'bring the temperature to about 225°F (107°C)'; cap 'under 250°F (121°C)'. 110°C/230°F is within range. 7h for a bone-in shank to pull-tender (interpolated from shoulder ~7h).

safe.ref: AmazingRibs — Food Temperature Guide (USDA whole-muscle lamb)
safe.note: Lamb listed at '145 (62.8)' °F(°C); USDA whole-muscle floor 145°F=63°C. Bath 74°C is well above floor. Displayed floor kept at 63°C per policy.

rationale: Kept all values: collagen shank at 74°C/24h is inside Baldwin's 55-80°C tough-cut window; smoke temps within AmazingRibs 107-121°C range; safe held at 63°C (AmazingRibs 145°F lamb). No reverse-order pasteurization data for a thick bone-in shank, so order_smokesv null.

sv.ref: Baldwin — Practical Guide to Sous Vide (barbecue ribs)
sv.note: Baldwin ribs: '176°F (80°C)... 8-12 hours or... 155°F (68°C) water bath for 24 hours'. 74°C/24h is a middle-ground within Baldwin's 68-80°C rib band; exact 74°C not a single quoted figure but inside range.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — Last Meal Ribs + Food Temp Guide
smoke.note: Hot finish ~110°C/230°F (within 'under 250°F' cap). tgt 95°C: 'we deliberately cook these meats up to 203°F' (203°F=95°C). Wood: 'I prefer chunks of apple, oak, or hickory for pork.'

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — Last Meal Ribs (pit temp + timing)
smoke_only.note: Corrected 115→107: canonical pit is 'about 225°F (107°C)'; 115°C/239°F was the set's high outlier though still under the 121°C cap. soh 5 confirmed: 'Allow 5 to 7 hours for... Spare Ribs.'

safe.note: 'Trichinosis is killed at 138°F and the new minimum recommended by USDA is 145°F' (145°F=63°C). Bath 74°C well above floor. Displayed floor kept at 63°C.

rationale: Corrected smoke-only pit 115→107°C to match AmazingRibs' cited 225°F/107°C (115°C was the outlier). Kept sv 74/24 (inside Baldwin 68-80°C rib band) and tgt 95 (=203°F). safe 63 documented. No by-thickness reverse-order data → order_smokesv null.

sv.ref: AmazingFoodMadeEasy — Sous Vide Pork Belly
sv.note: 72°C matches the 'more fall apart' belly band ('165°F for 1 to 2 Days (73.9°C)'). Baldwin has no belly-specific line. 12h is a shorter, leaner result than the source's 1-2 day recommendation (documented trade-off).

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — pork wood rec + general BBQ pit

smoke_only.note: 110°C/230°F within AmazingRibs pit range (107°C standard, 121°C cap). soh 4h plausible for 2kg belly; UNVERIFIED against a belly-specific tested recipe (AmazingRibs belly recipe is grilled, no smoke time).

safe.note: USDA whole-muscle pork 145°F=63°C; trichinosis killed at 138°F. Bath 72°C above floor. Displayed floor kept at 63°C.

sv.note: Baldwin traditional pork '141°F (61°C)'; 60°C/140°F is medium whole-muscle loin. 3-4h at thickness cooks and pasteurizes a loin. Kenji medium 140°F/60°C corroborates (Serious Eats blocked to fetcher, so search-only there).

safe.ref: AmazingRibs — Food Temperature Guide (USDA whole-muscle pork)

rationale: Lowered smoke-only pit 120→110°C toward AmazingRibs' cited 225°F/107°C standard (lean loin dries at 248°F). order_smokesv.note: Reverse order for an intact whole-muscle leg: cool/warm smoke raw (~50-60°C) for smoke ring, then sous-vide to full pasteurization. Baldwin Table 5.1, 70mm at 57°C = 5¼h (at 55°C = 6½h, 60°C = 4h). Keep total danger-zone time under Baldwin's 4h rule during the smoke phase.

rationale: Two corrections: tgt 60→57°C (60°C is 'Medium'; leg is served medium-rare, consistent with the 56°C bath) and smoke-only pit 120→107°C (AmazingRibs reverse-sears at 225°F). order_smokesv ENABLED for this intact whole-muscle leg using Baldwin Table 5.1 (70mm, 57°C = 5¼h). safe held at 63°C with the medium-rare pasteurization note.

smoke_only.note: 120°C/248°F is above the 107°C low-and-slow standard but within the 121°C cap — justified for rendering/crisping skin and cartilage on an ear. Documented as above-standard, not corrected.

safe.note: USDA whole-muscle pork 145°F=63°C. Ear taken well above for gelatinization. Displayed floor kept at 63°C.

rationale: Kept all values; 120°C smoke is defensible for crisping ear skin/cartilage (within AmazingRibs' 121°C cap). sv 75/8 and tgt 78 flagged UNVERIFIED against a named ear recipe (no canonical source located). safe 63 documented; order_smokesv null.

sv.note: 75°C/12h consistent with the collagen band (73.9-80°C, 12-24h) on the shank page. No tail-specific quoted figure — value UNVERIFIED against a named tail recipe.

smoke.note: High-heat finish 120°C to render fatty skin-on tail (within 121°C cap). tgt 80°C=176°F matches the 'Really Fall Apart: 176°F (80.0°C)' collagen endpoint. Wood apple.

smoke_only.note: 120°C/248°F above the 107°C standard but within the 121°C cap — justified for rendering a fatty skin-on tail. Documented as above-standard, not corrected.

safe.note: USDA whole-muscle pork 145°F=63°C. Tail taken to 80°C for gelatinization. Displayed floor kept at 63°C.

rationale: Kept all values; tgt 80°C matches the 'Really Fall Apart' 176°F collagen endpoint and 120°C smoke is defensible for a fatty tail (within cap). sv 75/12 flagged UNVERIFIED against a named tail recipe. safe 63 documented; order_smokesv null.
